channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas official issues warning after rancher killed by cartel IED in Mexico: 'A terrorist attack,' son says
item.description: A Texas rancher was killed by an explosive device on a road while traveling through Mexico earlier this month, officials said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Singer-musician Rhiannon Giddens calls off Kennedy Center show, citing Trump takeover
item.description: Award-winning singe-musician Rhiannon Giddens has become the latest artist to call off an appearance at the Kennedy Center, which has been in upheaval since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: Hillel Italie, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Denver man missing at Black Canyon in Gunnison National Park
item.description: The agencies are looking for the public's help in finding him. The missing man was identified as Jordan Marsters, 31, of Denver.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of beauty products for Black women contain health hazards
item.description: The study found 80% of more than 4,000 beauty products marketed to Black women have hazardous chemicals.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest Airlines plane has near-collision with jet at Midway Airport
item.description: A Southwest Airlines plane and private jet had a near-collision at Midway Airport in Chicago, Illinois on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Ponzio, NewsNation

item.title: Brian Laundrie's sister estranged from family amid explosive new docuseries
item.description: Brian Laundrie's sister Cassie says she hasn't spoken to her parents in 2 years and was misquoted by police in bodycam video replayed in a new docuseries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chris Jasper of the Isley Brothers dies at 73
item.description: Chris Jasper, a member of the Isley Brothers and Isley-Jasper-Isley, passed away on Sunday. He was 73 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Husband of Ruby Franke, Utah mommy blogger convicted of child abuse, has 'regrets' after wife's fall from fame
item.description: Utah parenting blogger Ruby Franke's husband says he has 'regrets' after his wife's descent into chaos. Ruby pleaded guilty to abusing two of her children in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live updates: House Republicans adopts budget after Trump whips GOP holdouts
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Denny's adding egg surcharge due to nationwide shortage
item.description: Denny's will be adding a surcharge for meals that include the use of eggs at some locations due to rising costs from a nationwide egg shortage, multiple outlets are reporting.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk extends deadline to describe work
item.description: More than two million federal workers remain uncertain about their job security after last night's deadline to respond to an email from Elon Musk, the man President Donald Trump has tapped to make the government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York inmate dies in prison as guards continue strike deemed illegal under state law
item.description: An inmate died in a New York prison as guards at prisons across the state continue their strike, which union officials acknowledge violates a state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Terrifying video gives inside look at what it would be like to be eaten by a shark
item.description: A tiger shark swallowed a camera, which gave divers an inside view of its mouth during a feeding, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experts warn of AI chatbots offering mental health advice
item.description: The American Psychological Association is warning about the growing use of chatbots "masquerading" as licensed mental health professionals. They cite two situations as examples.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Brazilian illegal alien charged with rape caught in Massachusetts, handed over to ICE in rare case
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Brazil, who entered the U.S. illegally in April 2024, was arrested last month in Falmouth, Massachusetts, and charged with rape.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A wildcat strike by guards at New York's prisons, now in its second week, is fueling fears over deteriorating conditions behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: List: Bay Area JOANN stores closing part of more than 500 shutting down nationwide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/KRON) — Fabric and crafts retailer Joann Inc., which has been a destination for generations of quilters, knitters and lovers of crafts projects for more than 80 years, is going out of business and shuttering all its stores.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Watch: Trump, Macron hold White House press conference
item.description: President Donald Trump hosts French President Emmanuel Macron on Monday, the first visit by a European leader to the White House since Trump returned.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by midday Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WGN 9

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9
